_about: Machine-readable assumptions registry seeded by A0 (de-risk spike). A8's preflight asserts env-support (halt loud + specific on unsupported), and the contract-guard validates Anthropic-side responses + creds shape at runtime (alarm + graceful-pause on drift). Decisions #29/#30/#31. Each entry: id, what, expected_contract, check (how to verify), supported_envs, implement_hint (what to build to support a new env), surface (preflight|contract|both).

assumptions.what: Host platform is one we have validated.
assumptions.expected_contract: $^O in {MSWin32, cygwin, msys} on host; linux inside the sandbox container.
assumptions.check: perl $^O / uname; membership test against supported_envs.
assumptions.implement_hint: Add a platform-support module entry (paths, shell, exec quoting, keep-awake API) for the new OS; until then preflight must halt loud naming the OS.

assumptions.what: HTTP transport that trusts the system cert store.
assumptions.expected_contract: curl present; on win32 a Schannel build (no CA file needed); HTTPS GET to api.anthropic.com succeeds.
assumptions.check: curl --version (look for Schannel on win32); a probe HTTPS request returns a TLS-valid response.
assumptions.implement_hint: Ensure curl (or an equivalent HTTPS client) is installed for the platform.

assumptions.what: Perl with HTTPS + JSON for the orchestrator/guard.
assumptions.expected_contract: perl >=5.14 with HTTP::Tiny, IO::Socket::SSL, JSON::PP loadable.
assumptions.implement_hint: Install perl + the three modules (all core/near-core).

assumptions.what: jq for hook JSON parsing (hooks fail-closed without it).
assumptions.expected_contract: jq present where butler hooks run (the sandbox).
assumptions.implement_hint: Install jq in the sandbox image; host hooks do not run by default.

assumptions.what: A CA bundle perl's IO::Socket::SSL can use (host perl lacks a default).
assumptions.expected_contract: A CA file path exists; on host /usr/ssl/certs/ca-bundle.crt.
assumptions.check: stat the candidate CA paths; assert at least one exists.
assumptions.implement_hint: Point SSL_ca_file at the platform CA bundle (or use a Schannel curl transport instead).

assumptions.expected_contract: ~/.claude/.credentials.json exists and is readable; mode 0600 (sandbox) / 0644 (cygwin-cosmetic on host).
assumptions.implement_hint: macOS stores creds in Keychain, not this file — a new code path is required there (currently unsupported).

assumptions.expected_contract: claudeAiOauth.{accessToken:str, refreshToken:str, expiresAt:int(epoch ms), scopes:[str], subscriptionType:str, rateLimitTier:str}
assumptions.check: parse + assert keys/types; expiresAt is epoch-ms (> 1e12).
assumptions.implement_hint: If Anthropic renames fields, update the contract + the write-back mapping; alarm on drift.

assumptions.expected_contract: GET https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage with Bearer + 'anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20' + 'User-Agent: claude-code/<ver>' returns 200 with five_hour.{utilization:int 0-100, resets_at:ISO-8601} and seven_day.{...}.
assumptions.check: one probe GET at launch; validate field presence/types; unauth returns 429 (not an auth signal).
assumptions.implement_hint: If the shape changes (renamed/missing field, non-200), the contract-guard alarms + graceful-pauses; pin the new shape here.

assumptions.expected_contract: POST https://platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token (Content-Type: application/json) body {grant_type:'refresh_token', refresh_token, client_id, scope} -> 200 {access_token, expires_in[, refresh_token]}. Premature refresh is rate-limited (429) by design.
assumptions.check: shape verified from Claude Code's own bundle; atomic write-back proven against a fixture; A8 ACTIVELY verifies one real in-band refresh near expiry and asserts 200 + contract.
assumptions.implement_hint: If the endpoint/domain/grant changes, alarm + pause; never assume success. Treat 429 as expected (backoff, in-band only).
assumptions.status: mechanism-proven; live-200 from our code not yet observed (blocked by premature-refresh 429); verified indirectly (Claude Code rotated the token).

assumptions.what: Public Claude Code OAuth client id.
assumptions.check: env override else the pinned default.
assumptions.implement_hint: If Anthropic rotates the public client id, update here; a 4xx invalid_client on refresh signals drift.

assumptions.what: Plugin PreToolUse hooks fire for a worker SUBAGENT's tool calls (containment + A4 gate depend on it).
assumptions.expected_contract: A settings/plugin PreToolUse hook fires on a subagent Edit/Write and can deny it; hook input carries agent_id/agent_type.
assumptions.check: SELF-TEST at preflight: dispatch a throwaway worker that attempts an out-of-scope edit; assert it is DENIED. Fail loud if containment is silently not firing.
assumptions.implement_hint: If a Claude Code version stops firing plugin hooks for subagents, containment is broken — halt loud; do not run a fleet.
assumptions.status: confirmed via docs + structural evidence; runtime self-assert is the durable proof (Decision #31).

assumptions.what: A long-blocking background command's completion re-invokes the (idle) interactive session.
assumptions.expected_contract: A detached command, on exit, delivers a notification that re-invokes the session (reporter auto-announce).
assumptions.check: PROVEN empirically (Probe 4). Robust fallback: bounded ~9-min long-poll + re-arm regardless.
assumptions.implement_hint: If re-invocation behavior changes, the bounded long-poll + re-arm still works; surface a warning.

assumptions.what: Container runtime for the sandbox surface.
assumptions.expected_contract: podman (or docker) present; sandbox image tag matches the Claude Code CLI version.
assumptions.implement_hint: Detect docker vs podman (already done elsewhere); on a host without either, dispatch-fleet is unsupported — halt loud.
